title: lithocarpus braianensis
intro: Lithocarpus braianensis, also known as the Bur Oak, is a type of deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus braianensis include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and treating digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Lithocarpus braianensis is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, and can also be consumed as a tea or capsule.
